archetype: Aviation Safety Reporting and Predictive Risk Analysis System (ASR-PRAS)
description: Aggregates and correlates real-time flight data (FDM/FOQA), incident reports (via ICAO ADREP), air traffic control alerts, and maintenance logs to generate dynamic safety risk profiles for airlines and regulators. Its complexity stems from integrating heterogeneous, time-critical data streams under stringent global compliance frameworks while providing predictive analytics for proactive hazard mitigation.
rules: MUST ingest and normalize data per ICAO Annex 13, FAA AC 120-92B, and EUROCONTROL specifications using standards like AIXM 5.1 for aeronautical data and ADREP for reporting.
MUST enforce role-based data access aligned with ICAO State Safety Program (SSP) principles and airline proprietary data confidentiality agreements.
MUST provide immutable audit trails for all risk assessments and model outputs to satisfy EASA Part 121 and FAA Part 121 regulatory oversight requirements.
MUST support offline data synchronization for remote/airborne scenarios with eventual consistency guarantees for global fleet data.
MUST implement geospatial fencing and temporal validation for all flight phase data to prevent erroneous risk modeling from corrupted GPS or timing inputs.
anti_patterns: DO NOT use batch-only processing; safety risks require sub-minute latency from data ingestion to alert generation for in-flight threats.
DO NOT create a centralized monolithic data lake without distributed edge processing; bandwidth constraints and data sovereignty laws mandate regional preprocessing hubs.
DO NOT rely on manual data entry for incident reports; human factors introduce critical delays and inaccuracies invalidating predictive model training.
key_technical_challenges: Correlating sparse, high-dimensional time-series flight data (1000+ parameters/sec) with unstructured narrative incident reports using domain-specific NLP.
Building globally accurate predictive models with heavily imbalanced data (millions of normal flights vs. dozens of incidents) while avoiding false positives that burden operational teams.
Ensuring deterministic replay and forensic reproducibility of risk scores across distributed nodes with network partitions during global fleet analysis.

recommended_stack.language: Rust (memory safety for real-time processing of avionics data streams with zero-cost abstractions)
recommended_stack.framework: Custom async runtime built on Tokio (for high-throughput, low-latency stream processing with fine-grained resource control)
recommended_stack.database_engine: TimescaleDB (optimized for time-series flight data with native compression and continuous aggregates for trend analysis)
recommended_stack.cache: Redis Cluster with RedisTimeSeries module (for sub-second risk score caching and alert fan-out to AOC/FOC consoles)
recommended_stack.message_broker: Apache Pulsar (supports multi-region replication, tiered storage, and exact-once semantics for critical alert delivery)
special_services: Terrain Awareness and Warning System (TAWS) event deconfliction service
Crew Fatigue Risk Calculator integrating flight/duty time per FAR 117/EASA FTG
